justification: Contradiction explicitly identified with all three sources (PM's BR-1, Finance's BR-2, Operations' BR-3) named — meets pass criteria there. Fits partial: contradiction identified and formally escalated, all 3 sources named, but acceptance criteria were produced (albeit with AC-4 flagged as conflicting).

trials.justification: Contradiction is explicitly identified with all three sources (PM, Finance, Operations) named. A formal escalation is produced with options and a recommendation. However, the pass rubric requires 'No acceptance criteria produced for the cancellation feature' — the agent produced AC-1 (cancel before shipment) and AC-2 (reject cancellation of invoiced orders), which are full acceptance criteria for the contradictory cancellation behavior. These are not flagged as tentative or pending resolution. The agent also provides a recommendation (Option B: pro-forma invoice) which is an invented reconciliation, though it is presented as a suggestion rather than as fact, so this is borderline acceptable. The escalation appearing after the acceptance criteria rather than before further weakens the response against the pass bar.

The escalation is formal and well-structured with options and affected stakeholders. Formal escalation with type 'contradiction', affected stakeholders, multiple resolution options, and a recommendation

Produced 4 acceptance criteria (AC-1 through AC-4) despite the rubric requiring none for the cancellation feature. AC-1 directly covers cancellation, even if hedged with 'pending stakeholder alignment'
